name: 24_Diabetes_Prediction_LogisticRegression_PimaIndians_ML
query: Configura un proyecto de predicción de diabetes utilizando un modelo de Regresión Logística y el conjunto de datos Pima Indians Diabetes. Realiza el escalado de características y la estandarización de datos en `src/data_loader.py`. Usa validación cruzada para evaluar el modelo en `src/train.py`, y guarda la puntuación de precisión en `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`. Genera y guarda la curva ROC en `results/figures/roc_curve.png`. Crea un panel interactivo usando Tableau o Power BI para mostrar el rendimiento del modelo y resaltar características importantes. Asegúrate de que el panel sea fácil de usar y documenta los pasos de procesamiento del conjunto de datos y creación de visualizaciones. Durante el desarrollo, el sistema debe gestionar automáticamente la apertura y cierre de Tableau o Power BI para evitar bloqueos innecesarios.

requirements.criteria: Se utiliza el conjunto de datos "Pima Indians Diabetes".
El escalado de características y la estandarización de datos se implementan en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se implementa un modelo de "Regresión Logística" en `src/model.py`.
Se utiliza validación cruzada para evaluar el modelo en `src/train.py`.
La puntuación de precisión se guarda en `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.
La curva ROC se genera y guarda como `results/figures/roc_curve.png`.
Se crea un panel de visualización interactivo usando "Tableau" o "Power BI" para mostrar el rendimiento del modelo y características importantes.

preferences.criteria: El panel debe permitir a los usuarios explorar diferentes aspectos del rendimiento del modelo y entender qué características contribuyen más a las predicciones.
El panel debe mostrar claramente cómo se procesó el conjunto de datos y cómo se crearon las visualizaciones.
Durante el desarrollo, el sistema debe abrir y cerrar automáticamente "Tableau" o "Power BI" según sea necesario para evitar largos períodos de bloqueo o inactividad.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utiliza el conjunto de datos "Pima Indians Diabetes".

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The code files (`data_loader.py`, `model.py`, `train.py`) are all minimal placeholder implementations that contain no actual loading, referencing, or processing of the "Pima Indians Diabetes" dataset. There is no reference to `diabetes.csv`, `pima-indians-diabetes.csv`, or any dataset URL/path. The `load_dataset()` function in `data_loader.py` simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", ...}` without any real data loading logic. The trajectory evidence also confirms no mention of this dataset was found.

judge_stats.criteria: El escalado de características y la estandarización de datos se implementan en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement any actual feature scaling or data standardization. It is a minimal placeholder/stub that only defines a `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` list (which merely lists the requirement as a string), a `load_dataset()` function that returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`, and a `build_transforms()` function that returns a static list `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]`. None of these functions perform real feature scaling (e.g., using `StandardScaler`, `MinMaxScaler`, or equivalent) or actual data standardization on any dataset. The file explicitly states it is a "minimal semantic implementation" placeholder, confirming no real functionality is present.

judge_stats.criteria: Se implementa un modelo de "Regresión Logística" en `src/model.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/model.py` file does not implement a Logistic Regression model. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub implementation with metadata strings and a `build_model()` function that returns a generic `"model placeholder"` string. There is no actual import or instantiation of a Logistic Regression classifier (e.g., `from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression`), no model training logic, and no real implementation of any machine learning model. The file appears to be a minimal semantic stub created for benchmark judging purposes rather than a genuine Logistic Regression implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utiliza validación cruzada para evaluar el modelo en `src/train.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/train.py` file contains only a minimal stub implementation that does not perform any actual machine learning. The `train()` function simply iterates over a range and accumulates a sum (`score += step`), with no dataset loading, model training, or cross-validation logic whatsoever. There is no use of `cross_val_score`, `KFold`, `StratifiedKFold`, or any other cross-validation mechanism. The file explicitly states it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" and only creates placeholder artifact files.

judge_stats.criteria: La puntuación de precisión se guarda en `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt` exists, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)` — a placeholder string rather than an actual accuracy score value. This indicates the file was not properly populated with a real computed accuracy metric from model evaluation.

judge_stats.criteria: La curva ROC se genera y guarda como `results/figures/roc_curve.png`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/roc_curve.png` exists in the project structure, as confirmed by the workspace tree. Although the image content could not be read due to a media type error in the evaluation tool (the file is a PNG but was attempted to be read as JPEG), the file itself is present at the expected path `results/figures/roc_curve.png`. The existence of the file in the directory structure satisfies the requirement that the ROC curve be generated and saved at that location.

judge_stats.criteria: Se crea un panel de visualización interactivo usando "Tableau" o "Power BI" para mostrar el rendimiento del modelo y características importantes.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The project files (`data_loader.py`, `model.py`, `train.py`) are minimal placeholder implementations with no Tableau or Power BI integration whatsoever. The only visualization artifact present is `results/figures/roc_curve.png`, which is generated as a plain text file by `train.py`'s `save_expected_artifacts()` function — not an interactive dashboard. There is no evidence of any Tableau workbook, Power BI report, data export for these tools, or any related configuration in the workspace.
